# MTL Per-Tokenizer Compression — LoRA Base-Model Study

<!-- GENERATED by bench/design-lora/tokcompress.py — do not edit by hand. -->

**Question.** MTL's economic thesis is that BPE tokenizers *merge* adjacent ASCII-punctuation glyphs, so a whitespace-free MTL program can cost **< 1 token per primitive glyph** and beat idiomatic (whitespace-separated) Python. That was only ever measured on OpenAI tiktoken. For a LoRA fine-tune we must know: **does the compression survive under the tokenizer of the open-weights base model we would train?** A base model whose BPE shreds ASCII punctuation changes the economics.

- Corpus: **35 paired MTL <-> idiomatic-Python programs** (19 glyph-dense `core` from `bench/corpus` via `tokcount/tasks.json`, newest MTL variant per task; 16 `tier3` capability programs from `bench/tier3/tasks` with their python twins).
- **Headline compression ratio = sum(Python tokens) / sum(MTL tokens) over the `core` group** (higher = MTL wins more; same string fed to every tokenizer). The glyph-dense `core` set is the one behind the repo's published 3.7-3.9x o200k numbers; `tier3` (named host words, ~1.9x) and the combined ratio are reported separately below.
- MTL primitive-glyph alphabet (25 single ASCII symbols): `[ ] : _ ~ @ ^ ! , ; ' + - * / % = < ? & . | > ( $`. Integer literals and whitespace are not counted as glyphs; tier-3 host-capability words (`readtext`, `emitint`, ...) are alphabetic and not glyphs.
- `tok/glyph (core)` = MTL tokens / primitive-glyph count over the `core` group only (the pure-glyph thesis set). **< 1.00 means the tokenizer achieves sub-token-per-primitive** — MTL's core claim.
- `frag% (core)` = share of `core` programs whose MTL token count **exceeds** their primitive-glyph count (per-program thesis-failure rate).
- Counting: one trailing newline stripped per file (matches `bench/tokcount`); HF encodes with `add_special_tokens=False`; tiktoken via `tiktoken==0.8.0`.
- `*` = tokenizer fetched from an ungated mirror because the vendor repo is gated; see provenance section. The tokenizer bytes are the base model's own.

## Headline table — `core` glyph-dense set (sorted by MTL compression ratio)

| StarCoder2-7B | StarCoder2 · BigCode-OpenRAIL-M | 141 | 555 | **3.94x** | 7.42 | 0.881 | 1.35 | 32% |
| Codestral-22B-v0.1 | Mistral/Codestral · MNPL (tokenizer ungated) | 167 | 639 | **3.83x** | 8.79 | 1.044 | 1.14 | 47% |
| Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 | Mistral · Apache-2.0 | 167 | 639 | **3.83x** | 8.79 | 1.044 | 1.14 | 47% |

### tier3 and combined ratios (same tokenizers)

`tier3` programs use alphabetic host-capability words, so MTL's compression there is smaller and the glyph metric does not apply; shown for completeness.

| Tokenizer | core ratio | tier3 ratio | combined ratio |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| CodeGemma-7B * | 4.75x | 2.50x | 3.60x |
| DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Lite | 4.46x | 2.32x | 3.37x |
| cl100k_base (GPT-4) | 4.01x | 1.83x | 2.90x |
| Llama-3.1-8B * | 4.01x | 1.83x | 2.90x |
| o200k_base (GPT-4o) | 3.97x | 1.83x | 2.89x |
| Qwen2.5-Coder-7B | 3.94x | 1.83x | 2.88x |
| StarCoder2-7B | 3.94x | 2.19x | 3.07x |
| Codestral-22B-v0.1 | 3.83x | 2.13x | 3.00x |
| Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 | 3.83x | 2.13x | 3.00x |

## Readout

Across **9 tokenizers** the MTL compression ratio spans **3.83x -- 4.75x** (a **1.24x spread**). Best: **CodeGemma-7B *** at **4.75x**. Worst: **Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3** at **3.83x**. The tiktoken **o200k_base** anchor lands at **3.97x** with **0.850** tokens/glyph on the core set — consistent with (marginally above, since the newest MTL variant per task is used here) the repo's published 3.72x/3.87x, and confirming the sub-1-token-per-glyph merging that is MTL's whole premise.

**Sub-1-token-per-glyph (MTL's edge intact):** CodeGemma-7B * (0.812), cl100k_base (GPT-4) (0.838), Llama-3.1-8B * (0.838), o200k_base (GPT-4o) (0.850), Qwen2.5-Coder-7B (0.856), DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Lite (0.875), StarCoder2-7B (0.881).

**>= 1 token per glyph (BPE fragments the glyphs):** Codestral-22B-v0.1 (1.044), Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 (1.044).

**Primary LoRA candidate — Qwen2.5-Coder-7B:** ratio **3.94x**, **0.856** tokens/glyph, 1.39 bytes/token, 16% of core programs fragmented — it **PRESERVES** MTL's sub-token-per-glyph edge. Every candidate still keeps MTL well ahead of Python (ratio > 1) even where glyph merging is weaker, but the economics are strongest where tok/glyph stays below 1.

## Cold-preamble (quickref) token cost per tokenizer

`docs/mtl-quickref.md` is the cold in-context-learning preamble a non-fine-tuned MTL agent must pay every session. A LoRA fine-tune's whole point is to retire this fixed cost, but its size per tokenizer matters for any cold-start comparison.

| Tokenizer | quickref tokens |
| --- | ---: |
| Llama-3.1-8B * | 4036 |
| cl100k_base (GPT-4) | 4037 |
| Qwen2.5-Coder-7B | 4046 |
| o200k_base (GPT-4o) | 4051 |
| StarCoder2-7B | 4169 |
| CodeGemma-7B * | 4214 |
| DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Lite | 4447 |
| Codestral-22B-v0.1 | 4619 |
| Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 | 4619 |

## Method notes

- **Pairing** reuses `bench/tokcount/tasks.json` (the same MTL<->Python mapping behind the repo's published 3.72x/3.87x numbers). For each corpus task the newest MTL variant present is used (v0.3 > v0.2 > v0.1); tasks with no MTL solution (inexpressible `two_sum`, `binary_search`) are skipped.
- **tier3** programs contain named host-capability words, so their tok/glyph is not meaningful; the headline ratio and every glyph column are computed over the `core` group only, with tier3 and combined ratios shown separately.
- Ratios are token-for-token on identical source bytes; no model weights are downloaded, only tokenizer files.

Corpus: 35 pairs (19 core, 16 tier3). Tokenizers attempted: 9; succeeded: 9; failed: 0.
